vulnerability.reference: Lack of domain-specific validation for reward calibration on rare side effects in medical data.
vulnerability.generated: Clinicians and patients lack independent verification of AI-generated summaries, relying entirely on them for comprehensive side-effect information.

impact.reference: Clinicians miss critical side-effect information, leading to adverse drug events and patient harm.
impact.generated: Omission of critical side-effect mentions causes uninformed prescribing decisions, leading to preventable adverse drug events and patient harm.

dose_response.reference: In federated medical text summarization, federated DPO degrades reward calibration, enabling omission of critical side-effect mentions, exposing clinicians to unrecognized adverse events, leading to patient harm and eroded trust.
dose_response.generated: Federated DPO with differential privacy degrading reward model calibration, through clinicians and patients relying on AI-generated medical summaries, triggers lack of independent verification, leading to omission of critical side-effect mentions causing uninformed prescribing decisions and preventable adverse drug events.
